description: Accounts in Judaeo-Arabic with calculations that draw on both the Hebrew alphanumerical and Arabic numerical systems. The paleography suggests that these accounts date to the fifteenth or sixteenth centuries at the earliest. The second entry on the recto's right bifolium titled "expenses" (מצרוף) lists several full names including: Shelomo Zimra (a common Sephardi variation of Zamero), Shelomo b. Ṣōr[?], and Avraham b. Ṣōr[?] (ן׳ צור). Among the entries on the recto's left bifolium we find the names: Sheḥata b. Yōsef Ṣīyād (Fisherman), Aḥmad Jdawī, Shwīsh Ḥasan (שויש is possibly the Turkish rank Çavuş), Aḥmad b. Muḥammad דלגה (this latter word may be Turkish for a certain profession). Unfortunately, none of the account entries list a numismatic term that would help ascertain the chronology of the when these accounts were issued. MCD.
description_he: חשבונות בערבית-יהודית עם חישובים בשיטות אלפאנומרית עברית וספרות ערביות; כנראה מאות 15-16; שמות: שלמה זמרה, שלמה בן צור, אברהם בן צור, שחאתה בן יוסף הצייד, אחמד ג'דאוי, שויש חסן
